issues.title: RFIs page missing Ball In Court tracking and overdue detection per acceptance criteria
issues.description: PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA requires Ball In Court (BIC) color-coded indicator, overdue detection with red OVERDUE badge, Days Open calculation, and KPI bar showing total open/overdue/avg days to close. Currently the RFIs page lacks BIC column, overdue highlighting, and computed KPI metrics. These are P0 acceptance test failures.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/RFIs.tsx, make these specific changes: Add a KPI bar section at the top of the page with four MetricBox cards: Total Open (count where status !== 'closed'), Overdue (count where due_date < today AND status !== 'closed'), Avg Days to Close (mean of closed_at minus created_at for closed RFIs), and Closed This Week. Add a 'Days Open' computed column to the table that calculates dayjs diff between now and created_at for open RFIs, or closed_at minus created_at for closed ones. Add a 'Ball In Court' column showing assigned_to name with a colored dot (blue for GC, purple for Architect, orange for Owner). Add overdue row highlighting: if due_date is before today and status is not closed, render the due date cell text in theme.colors.error red and show a small 'OVERDUE' Tag badge. BEFORE: RFI list shows basic columns without BIC, days open, overdue badges, or KPI summary cards. AFTER: RFI list has 4 KPI metric cards at top, Days Open column, Ball In Court column with color dots, and overdue rows highlighted with red due date text and OVERDUE badge. Use the existing MetricBox and Tag components from Primitives. Calculate all metrics from the fetched RFI data array. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Submittals page missing approval chain visualization and spec section tracking
issues.description: PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA requires approval chain horizontal stepper showing each reviewer with decision status, spec section column in CSI format, revision number tracking, and lead time calculation. The current submittals page lacks the approval workflow stepper, spec section display, and lead time warnings. These are core submittal register requirements.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/Submittals.tsx, make these specific changes: Add a 'Spec Section' column to the submittals table that displays the specification_section field (e.g. '03 30 00'). Add a 'Revision' column showing revision_number with 'Rev' prefix (e.g. 'Rev 0', 'Rev 1'). Add a 'Lead Time' column that calculates business days between submission date and today for pending submittals, showing amber text if > 14 days and red if > 21 days. Add KPI metric cards at top: Total Submittals, Pending Review (status in submitted or review_in_progress), Approved count, and Resubmission Rate (percentage with revision > 0). In the detail panel or row expansion, add a simple horizontal stepper component showing the approval chain: Subcontractor → GC Review → Architect Review with status dots (green for approved, amber for pending, red for rejected) at each step based on the submittal status. BEFORE: Submittals table shows basic columns without spec section, revision tracking, lead time, or approval chain visualization. AFTER: Table includes Spec Section, Revision, and Lead Time columns with color-coded warnings, 4 KPI cards at top, and a horizontal approval chain stepper in the detail view. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Change Orders page missing three-tier PCO/COR/CO pipeline and cost breakdown
issues.description: PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA requires CO numbers sequential and immutable, cost breakdown by type (labor/material/equipment/sub with markup), schedule impact field, running total of approved COs showing net contract change, and PCO to CO promotion workflow. Currently the change orders page lacks the three-tier pipeline view, markup calculation, and contract impact summary.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/ChangeOrders.tsx, make these specific changes: Add a summary bar at the top with 5 metric cards: Total COs, Approved Total (sum of cost_impact where status is approved formatted as currency), Pending Total (sum where status is pending), Rejected Total, and Net Contract Change (approved total as percentage of original contract). Add a 'Type' column showing co_type with colored Tag badges: PCO in amber, COR in blue, CO in green. Add an 'Amount' column displaying total_cost formatted as USD currency with positive amounts in red and negative in green. Add a 'Schedule Impact' column showing total_time_impact as '+X days' or 'No impact'. Add filter tabs at the top: All, PCO, COR, CO that filter by co_type. Ensure the table rows are clickable and show a detail panel with the cost breakdown line items including description, quantity, unit_price, and total_price. BEFORE: Change orders page shows a basic list without type differentiation, cost formatting, schedule impact, or summary metrics. AFTER: Page has 5 KPI summary cards, type filter tabs, color-coded type badges, formatted currency amounts, schedule impact column, and a detail panel with line item cost breakdown. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Punch List missing two-step verification workflow and before/after photo comparison
issues.description: PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA requires two-step verification (Sub marks Complete → GC Verifies or Rejects), status pipeline Open → In Progress → Sub Complete → Verified, responsible party color coding, due date aging colors, and before/after photo side-by-side. The punch list currently lacks the verification workflow buttons and photo comparison UI.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/PunchList.tsx, make these specific changes: Update the status column to use the four-stage pipeline with colored dots: Open (amber), In Progress (blue), Sub Complete (purple), Verified (green). For items in 'Sub Complete' status, show a 'Verify' button (green) and a 'Reject' button (red) visible only to project_manager+ roles. Clicking Verify changes status to 'verified' with verified_at timestamp. Clicking Reject opens a small inline text input for rejection reason, then sets status back to 'open'. Add due date aging: if due_date is more than 4 days away show green text, 1 to 4 days show amber, overdue show red with 'X days overdue' label. Add responsible party column with colored background tint: warm/orange for Subcontractor trade, blue for GC items, neutral for Owner. Add KPI stat bar: Total Items, Open count, Awaiting Verification (sub_complete count), Verified count, and Overdue count. BEFORE: Punch list shows basic status without verification workflow, no due date aging colors, no responsible party distinction, no KPI cards. AFTER: Four-stage status pipeline with Verify/Reject action buttons on Sub Complete items, color-coded due date aging, responsible party backgrounds, and 5 KPI metric cards at top. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: All core workflow pages missing ARIA labels, keyboard navigation, and empty state context
issues.description: Global acceptance criteria require all pages pass axe-core with zero critical violations, keyboard navigable tab order, aria-labels on all icons and status indicators, aria-live regions for status changes, and contextual empty states. RFIs page (representative of all core workflow pages) lacks role attributes on table, aria-labels on action buttons, and shows generic empty state instead of contextual one with specific CTA.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/RFIs.tsx, make these specific changes: Add role='table' to the main data table container and role='row' to each table row. Add aria-label attributes to all icon-only buttons (e.g. filter icon gets aria-label='Filter RFIs', sort icons get aria-label='Sort by [column name]'). Add aria-live='polite' to the KPI metric cards container so screen readers announce when counts update. Add aria-label to each status Tag/badge (e.g. aria-label='Status: Open'). For the empty state when no RFIs exist, replace any generic 'No data found' text with: a FileQuestion icon (from lucide-react), the text 'No RFIs have been created for this project yet', a subtitle 'When questions arise in the field, create an RFI to get a documented answer', and a primary action Button 'Create First RFI'. For filtered-empty state (filters active but no results), show 'No RFIs match your current filters' with a 'Clear Filters' button. Add tabIndex={0} to each clickable table row and onKeyDown handler that opens detail on Enter key press. BEFORE: Table lacks ARIA roles, buttons lack aria-labels, empty state is generic or missing, no keyboard navigation on rows. AFTER: Full ARIA markup on table structure, labeled buttons and status indicators, contextual empty states with specific CTAs, and keyboard-navigable rows. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

competitive_intel: Procore's RFI module has ball-in-court tracking, email reply integration, and response time analytics built in. Their submittal register tracks spec sections with multi-party approval chains. Fieldwire excels at punch list with photo-first mobile capture and two-step verification. SiteSync's core workflow pages currently function as basic CRUD lists without the construction-specific workflow intelligence (BIC, approval chains, verification steps, cost breakdowns) that GCs require daily. The gap is not in data storage but in domain-specific UI patterns that make these workflows actionable.
summary: Core Workflows scores 24/100 primarily because the four critical pages (RFIs, Submittals, Change Orders, Punch List) function as generic list/detail views rather than construction-specific workflow tools. Against PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA, every page fails multiple P0 test cases: RFIs lack Ball In Court tracking and overdue detection, Submittals lack approval chain visualization and spec section tracking, Change Orders lack the three-tier PCO/COR/CO pipeline with cost breakdown and markup calculation, and Punch List lacks the two-step verification workflow. All pages also fail global acceptance criteria for ARIA attributes, keyboard navigation, and contextual empty states. The API layer in src/api/endpoints has solid TypeScript patterns and error handling, but the page components do not surface the domain-rich computed metrics (days open, lead time, contract impact, aging) that construction professionals need. The five prompts target adding construction-specific KPI cards, workflow-specific columns, status pipelines, and accessibility markup to each core page.
